electroacoustic.max_spl_note: Peak SPL per amplified controller, measured at 1 m under free-field with crest-factor-4 pink noise, [K1] preset.
electroacoustic.directivity: 90 degrees symmetric horizontal; vertical coverage set by number of elements and array curvature
electroacoustic.components: LF: 2 x 15" neodymium (bass-reflex); MF: 4 x 6.5" neodymium direct-radiating (bass-reflex); HF: 3 x 3" diaphragm compression drivers on DOSC waveguide. K-shape transducer layout, 3-way active.

operating_modes: With K1-SB for rear noise control (K1SB_100_NC)
amplification.amplification_note: Quad-amplified by LA12X; one LA12X drives 2 K1 enclosures (4 outputs each: 2 LF, 1 MF, 1 HF). LA-RAK II AVB houses three LA12X.

connection.breakouts: DOSUB-LA8 (8-pt PA-COM to 4x 2-pt speakON for passive subs), DOFILL-LA8 (8-pt PA-COM to 2x 4-pt speakON for 2-way active), DO3WFILL (8-pt PA-COM to 1x 4-pt speakON + 2x 2-pt speakON)
connection.internal_pinout: PA-COM A/B = left LF, C/D = right LF, E/F = MF, G/H = HF

physical.cabinet: Premium grade Baltic birch plywood with high-density polyethylene; coated steel grill with acoustically neutral 3D fabric; high grade anti-corrosion steel rigging; finish grey brown RAL 8019

deployment.max_per_flown_array: 16 K1 safe limit, 24 K1 maximum limit with K1-BUMP (14 K1 + 3 LA-RAK II AVB safe / 23 K1 + 4 LA-RAK II AVB maximum when racks are flown on the array)
rigging_accessories: KARA-DOWNK1 (Kara II downfill bumper, up to 6 Kara II)
K2-LINK (rear attachment of K2 below K1)

usage: Flagship long-throw WST line source for large-scale touring and arena/festival main hangs where consistent 90-degree symmetric horizontal coverage and long throw are needed.
Power-dense: each cabinet needs 4 amp channels, so one LA12X drives 2 K1 and a 3-up LA-RAK II AVB powers 6 K1 - budget amp inventory in pairs of cabinets.
Pair with low end for full-range output: K1-SB for tight low extension (K1SB_60), or KS28 / CS1 cardioid subs; the recommended K1-SB ratio is 2 K1 : 1 K1-SB integrated within the hang.
Use the K1SB_100_NC preset when rear/backstage SPL rejection matters - it trades a little front output for broadband cardioid cancellation behind the array.

Inter-element angles are limited to 5 degrees max, so K1 is best for medium-to-long throw zones; pair shorter-throw boxes (Kara II via KARA-DOWNK1, or K2 via K2-LINK) underneath for near-field downfill.

Mind the rigging limits: flown arrays are capped at 16 cabinets (safe) / 24 (maximum), and IP43 protection means it is suited to covered or short-term outdoor use rather than permanent weather exposure - always validate any array in Soundvision.
